title: Magical Silky Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: This herbal and adventurous Moroccan dish features callaloo and binchotan-charcoal for a unique flavor, and is low in sodium for a healthier option.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the chopped callaloo, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the tagine and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
Add the binchotan charcoal to the tagine and cover with a lid to smoke the dish for 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
